summary: Ukrainian forces report multiple incursions of Russian Shahed drones and strike‑UAVs across central and eastern oblasts, while Russia prepares a new wave of missile‑drone strikes targeting decision‑making centers. A significant prisoner‑exchange returned 205 Ukrainian servicemen, and Russia is reportedly mobilising MiG‑31K fighters equipped with Kinzhal hypersonic missiles. International diplomatic activity continues with EU‑backed war‑crimes tribunal preparations and increased Western financial support.
key_points: Shahed and strike‑UAV activity intensifies across Cherkasy, Odesa, Sumy and Donetsk regions.
MiG‑31K fighters with Kinzhal missiles launched from Savasianka airfield.
Russia plans new missile‑drone attacks on Ukrainian decision‑making hubs.
205 Ukrainian POWs returned in the latest exchange; 41 Russian dead transferred.
EU and 36 nations approve a special tribunal to prosecute Russian aggression.
IMF mission to review Ukraine's $8.1 bn loan program scheduled for June.
Civil unrest in Odesa over mobilisation‑related TCC vehicle blockade.

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian radar networks have logged a surge of Russian Shahed drones and strike‑UAV groups moving southwest across Cherkasy, Odesa and Donetsk, threatening civilian and military infrastructure. Concurrently, Russia has deployed MiG‑31K interceptors armed with Kinzhal hypersonic missiles from Savasianka, signalling a potential escalation in air‑strike capability. Diplomatic and humanitarian developments include a prisoner exchange of 205 Ukrainian soldiers and the EU‑backed establishment of a special war‑crimes tribunal.

points: Shahed drones were observed near Dnipro railway facilities, with four units passing north of Zolotonosha in Cherkasy, heading south‑west (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66762).
A Shahed group entered Odesa region, targeting the town of Izmail; residents were urged to seek shelters (Source: @kpszsu/61995).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed strike near the village of Zguriivka in Kyiv oblast, moving toward Brovary (Source: @kpszsu/62002).
In Sumy oblast, multiple UAV groups were detected moving westward from Sumy city toward Poltava, indicating a coordinated strike pattern (Source: @kpszsu/61981).
A Shahed drone approached Kyiv from the north, heading toward the Vinogradar district and Minsk area, prompting heightened alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66749).
Ukrainian air defenses engaged a Shahed over Dnipro railway station, with acoustic detection confirming the hit (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66734).
Ukrainian authorities confirmed the return of 205 POWs, including personnel from all major service branches, after the first phase of the 1000‑on‑1000 exchange (Source: @DeepStateUA/23506).
Russia launched MiG‑31K fighters from Savasianka airfield, each capable of carrying the hypersonic Kinzhal (H‑47M2) missile, a system noted for low interception rates (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66757).
Russian forces announced a new missile‑drone campaign aimed at Ukrainian "centers of decision‑making," targeting roughly two dozen political and military sites (Source: @UkraineNow/81239).
A Russian Shahed group entered Donetsk oblast near Sloviansk, heading toward Pavlohrad, indicating a push into the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/62080).
Russian air‑defence reported a nationwide alert after the launch of a MiG‑31K equipped with Kinzhal missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/91942).
Russian forces claimed the destruction of a Ukrainian Be‑200 amphibious aircraft and a Ka‑27 helicopter in the Sea of Azov region (Source: @UkraineNow/81214).
Russian intelligence warned of possible ballistic missile use on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/61956).
Russia exchanged 41 of its fallen fighters for 526 Ukrainian bodies, reflecting ongoing POW negotiations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30187).
Shahed groups were sighted over Donetsk region near Sloviansk, moving southeast toward Pavlohrad, suggesting pressure on the eastern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62080).
Ukrainian radar logged a Shahed swarm near the town of Zolotonosha in Cherkasy, heading toward the Dnipro River, potentially to disrupt logistics (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66762).
A Shahed drone was reported over the Kharkiv‑Luhansk border area, indicating continued cross‑border UAV activity (Source: @kpszsu/61989).
Russian forces claimed a successful strike on a Ukrainian train near Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81213).
Ukrainian units reported a Shahed attack near the village of Zguriivka, Kyiv oblast, threatening the Brovary corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62002).
Russian artillery fire hit the Ryazan oil refinery, a strategic target in the western sector of the eastern front (Source: @UkraineNow/81247).
Ukrainian forces observed a new UAV group entering Sumy oblast from the west, moving toward Poltava, potentially to support eastern operations (Source: @kpszsu/61981).
A Shahed drone was detected over Chernihiv oblast near Varva, heading toward Poltava, indicating north‑south UAV penetration (Source: @kpszsu/61997).
Russian ballistic missile threat warnings were issued for the northern sector, though no launch was confirmed (Source: @kpszsu/61956).
MiG‑31K fighters equipped with Kinzhal missiles were launched from Savasianka, a northern‑located airfield, raising the threat to Kyiv and surrounding areas (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66757).
Ukrainian radar reported a Shahed over the Kyiv region heading toward the Vinogradar district, prompting city‑wide alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66749).
A Shahed was observed near the village of Zguriivka, moving toward Brovary, suggesting a possible strike corridor toward the capital (Source: @kpszsu/62002).
Ukrainian forces reported acoustic detection of a Shahed over Dnipro railway, a key northern logistics hub (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66734).
Russian air‑defence announced a nationwide alert after Kinzhal‑capable MiG‑31K launch, affecting northern air‑space monitoring (Source: @eRadarrua/91942).
Shahed groups entered Odesa region targeting Izmail, prompting civilian shelter orders (Source: @kpszsu/61995).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed near the town of Zolotonosha moving southwest, potentially threatening the southern Dnipro corridor (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66762).
Russian forces claimed destruction of a Be‑200 amphibious aircraft and a Ka‑27 helicopter in the Sea of Azov, indicating intensified naval‑air activity (Source: @UkraineNow/81214).
A Shahed strike hit near the village of Zguriivka in Kyiv oblast, with the flight path directed toward Brovary, a gateway to southern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/62002).
Ukrainian radar logged a Shahed over the Kherson‑Mykolaiv border area, suggesting pressure on the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/61994).
Russian missile components identified in Kyiv attacks were traced to Western manufacturers, highlighting supply‑chain vulnerabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81246).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed over the Dnipro railway station, a critical southern logistics hub (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66734).
MiG‑31K fighters equipped with Kinzhal hypersonic missiles launched from Savasianka, a platform capable of striking deep into Ukrainian territory (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66757).
Ukrainian radar detected multiple Shahed drone swarms across central and southern Ukraine, with at least four groups moving toward Kyiv, Odesa and Donetsk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66762, @kpszsu/61995).
Russian air‑defence announced a nationwide alert after the Kinzhal‑capable MiG‑31K launch, indicating heightened air‑space tension (Source: @eRadarrua/91942).
Ukrainian forces reported acoustic detection of a Shahed over Dnipro railway, confirming active UAV engagement (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66734).
Russian forces claimed the downing of a Ukrainian Be‑200 amphibious aircraft and a Ka‑27 helicopter in the Sea of Azov (Source: @UkraineNow/81214).
Ukraine reported a Shahed approaching Kyiv from the north toward Vinogradar district, prompting city‑wide alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66749).
Russian missile components recovered from Kyiv attacks were traced to Western manufacturers, suggesting continued external supply to Russian missile programs (Source: @UkraineNow/81246).

key_developments: MiG‑31K fighters armed with Kinzhal hypersonic missiles launched from Savasianka airfield, expanding Russia's long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66757).
Russian Shahed drone groups penetrated central Ukraine, with four units passing north of Zolotonosha heading southwest, threatening logistics corridors (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66762).
A new Russian missile‑drone campaign targets Ukrainian decision‑making centers, focusing on roughly two dozen political and military sites (Source: @UkraineNow/81239).
205 Ukrainian POWs returned in the latest exchange, while Russia repatriated 41 of its dead, reflecting ongoing negotiation dynamics (Source: @DeepStateUA/23506).
EU and 36 nations approved a special tribunal to prosecute Russian aggression, marking a significant legal escalation (Source: @UkraineNow/81247).
Russia announced a nationwide alert after launching Kinzhal‑capable MiG‑31K, indicating heightened air‑defence readiness across Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/91942).
Shahed drones were observed over Odesa region near Izmail, prompting civilian shelter orders and highlighting southern front pressure (Source: @kpszsu/61995).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV and missile activity is intensifying across multiple fronts, with Shahed swarms threatening both civilian infrastructure and supply lines in central, southern, and northern Ukraine. The deployment of MiG‑31K fighters carrying Kinzhal hypersonic missiles represents a significant escalation in strike depth, potentially overwhelming existing air‑defence systems. Continued Russian focus on Ukrainian decision‑making hubs suggests a strategic attempt to disrupt command and control, while diplomatic efforts such as the EU‑backed tribunal may increase political pressure on Moscow.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing air‑defence alerts in Kyiv, Odesa and Donetsk, reallocating mobile radar units to track low‑altitude Shahed incursions. Western partners are reviewing the $8.1 bn IMF loan framework and supplying advanced air‑defence components, as evidenced by the identification of Western microchips in captured missiles. Regional authorities are coordinating civilian evacuations and shelter protocols amid the heightened drone threat.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring indicates a pattern of coordinated UAV groups moving from Russian‑held territories into Ukrainian rear areas, likely to test air‑defence gaps before larger missile strikes. The Kinzhal‑capable MiG‑31K launch suggests Russia may be preparing a limited series of high‑value strikes, possibly targeting command nodes identified in the recent Russian threat brief. Continued diplomatic activity, including the prisoner exchange and tribunal formation, may be leveraged by both sides to gain operational concessions.
